scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured tool outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within specialized research tooling scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured tool outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Field Input Normalization section added with alias mapping table. references/fellowships.md created with full fellowship details. Covers filtering, ranking, and deadline display.

reliability.note: Error handling documented; missing-field prompts present; field normalization now handles common aliases. Minor: no explicit handling for unrecognized nationality codes.

performance_context.note: references/fellowships.md now holds full fellowship details — good progressive disclosure. SKILL.md remains concise.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear; stress-case rules defined; feedback design adequate; field normalization documented. Input Validation redirect now includes specific alternative (postdoc office).

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable for postdoc applicants. Field normalization improves forgiveness for common aliases.

security.note: No credentials required; input validation present; no PII risk beyond optional applicant name.

maintainability.note: Fellowship database now in references/fellowships.md — updates no longer require editing SKILL.md. Clean structure.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; progressive disclosure via references/fellowships.md; composability still limited — no structured output schema.

inputs.note: Field normalization handles 'immuno' → 'immunology'. Fellowship details now in references/fellowships.md with official URLs.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate fellowship deadlines or eligibility rules
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.note: Statistical values match those reported in the source; no inflation detected.

assertions.text: Output does not exceed scope by drafting application materials
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.note: Skill correctly refuses and now suggests consulting institution's postdoc office as alternative.

assertions.note: Request appropriately declined with an explanatory message.

assertions.text: Refusal message references the correct scope boundary
assertions.note: Request appropriately declined with an explanatory message.

assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.text: Output suggests an appropriate alternative action or resource
assertions.note: Refusal now directs user to academic writing skill or institution's postdoc office

key_strengths: Fellowship database moved to references/fellowships.md — updates no longer require editing SKILL.md, significantly improving maintainability
Field Input Normalization section with alias mapping table resolves the fuzzy matching gap from v1
Out-of-scope refusal now includes specific actionable alternative (academic writing skill or postdoc office)
Clear three-parameter filter model (nationality, years-since-PhD, field) is well-matched to the task

recommendations.title: No explicit handling for unrecognized nationality codes
recommendations.problem: The skill accepts nationality as a free-text string (e.g., 'US', 'CN') but provides no documented behavior for unrecognized codes (e.g., 'American', 'Chinese', 'UK').
recommendations.root_cause: No nationality normalization table or validation step is documented in the skill.
recommendations.fix: Add a nationality normalization table or accepted-values list to the parameters section, and document common aliases (e.g., 'American' → 'US', 'Chinese' → 'CN') in SKILL.md or references/fellowships.md.

recommendations.title: No structured output schema for downstream integration
recommendations.problem: The skill outputs human-readable text but provides no structured JSON output option, limiting integration with application tracking tools.
recommendations.root_cause: Output format is not parameterized; no --format flag or output schema is documented.
recommendations.fix: Add an optional --format json flag and document the JSON output schema in SKILL.md or references/ to enable downstream integration.
